compiled_state.title: As a ticket system consumer, I can record file impact data as a structured event on a ticket
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Add FILE_IMPACT to ticket-lib.sh write_commit_event() event type enum. Update ticket-reducer.py to compile FILE_IMPACT events into a structured file_impact field (array of {path, reason} objects) in the ticket's compiled state.

**Why**: Establishes the structured-data foundation that replaces ad-hoc markdown parsing by 4 downstream scripts. Without this event type, nothing else in the epic can proceed.

**Scope**:
- IN: ticket-lib.sh write_commit_event() enum extension, ticket-reducer.py compilation logic producing file_impact as an array of {path, reason} objects in compiled state, unit tests for both
- OUT: CLI subcommands (Story B); Jira bridge (Stories C, D); data migration (Story E); consumer refactors (Story F)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, ticket-lib.sh write_commit_event() accepts FILE_IMPACT in its event type enum ← Satisfies: "A FILE_IMPACT event type is supported by the ticket system: ticket-lib.sh write_commit_event() accepts FILE_IMPACT in its event type enum"
- When this story is complete, ticket-reducer.py compiles FILE_IMPACT events into a structured file_impact field containing an array of {path, reason} objects in the ticket's compiled state ← Satisfies: "ticket-reducer.py compiles FILE_IMPACT events into a structured file_impact field in the ticket's compiled state — containing an array of {path, reason} objects"
- When this story is complete, unit tests pass for all new or modified logic in both ticket-lib.sh and ticket-reducer.py

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] FILE_IMPACT event follows the existing event pattern (JSON file in ticket directory, atomic commit via flock)
- [Testing] Unit tests should cover: enum acceptance, reducer compilation of multiple FILE_IMPACT events (latest wins vs. accumulation — choose semantic and document), malformed payload handling

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Done definition update: Specify FILE_IMPACT conflict-resolution semantics in ticket-reducer.py — apply latest-wins: the last FILE_IMPACT event by timestamp replaces the compiled file_impact array entirely; earlier events are discarded. Document this in reducer comments and in ticket-system-v3-architecture.md (via docs story 4610-cb5e). This semantic must be fixed before stories 54ee-c932 (migration) and 2998-a1ba (consumer refactors) so those stories can reason about output correctness. [adversarial-review, blue-team accepted]
comments.author: Claude
